entities.description: information foraging theory  ift  is a framework for understanding information access as a process analogous to animal foraging
animal foraging is the process used as an analogy to explain how users access information in the context of information foraging theory
information scent refers to cues like keywords or icons that users follow to navigate content
information patches are clusters of content  such as sections in handbooks  that users navigate between
handbooks are mentioned as containing sections that serve as information patches
keywords are identified as specific examples of information scent cues used by users
icons are identified as specific examples of information scent cues used by users
sections are described as parts of handbooks that function as information patches
reference 42 is the citation source for information foraging theory mentioned in the text

relations.description: information foraging theory uses animal foraging as an analogy to explain information access
information foraging theory suggests that users follow information scent cues to navigate content
information foraging theory describes information patches as clusters of content that users navigate between
information scent cues like keywords or icons are found within sections of handbooks  which act as information patches
keywords are explicitly listed as examples of information scent
icons are explicitly listed as examples of information scent
sections in handbooks are explicitly listed as examples of information patches
information foraging theory is cited with reference number 42 in the text
